preview_text:
The model favors Colombia heavily, assigning an 86% win probability and leaving Ghana with virtually no chance. The underdog is estimated to secure a draw in 14% of simulations, while the model rules out an outright Ghana victory in its forecast.

The W/D/L spread reflects a large gap between the favorite and draw outcome. Colombia's dominant modeled edge means a decisive result is the likeliest scenario, though the 14% draw probability indicates the match retains some competitive structure rather than appearing predetermined. A Ghana win sits outside the model's credible range.

A meaningful divergence separates model and market. The sportsbook consensus assigns Colombia only 67%, while assigning Ghana a 11% chance of victory—a gap of roughly 19 percentage points on the win market. Polymarket prices align more closely with the books than with the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the scale of disagreement warrants attention from users evaluating this fixture.
